headline: For more than a year, Republican senators had largely been deferential to President Trump‘s wishes. This week, they revolted over a Trump endorsement and his $1.776 billion ‘anti-weaponization fund.’
Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
Supreme Court revives large Cuba property claims against cruise lines as Trump escalates pressure on Havana and Raúl Castro
EU negotiators reached a provisional deal to remove some tariffs on U.S. imports as part of the bloc’s trade deal signed last summer, ahead of a U.S. deadline to ramp up tariffs on cars
Spencer Pratt, the former reality TV star of “The Hills,” is running a biting, social-media-savvy campaign to be the next mayor of Los Angeles
Congo residents set fire to Ebola treatment center as fear, anger grow over outbreak
Dow soars 300 points to an all-time high — first record hit since Iran war began
Hilton and Becerra lead in a tightening race in final weeks of California governor's campaign, poll shows
Kevin Warsh is to be sworn in as Fed chairman on Friday, and some investors say the central bank’s next move could be a rate hike—not the cut he was hired to deliver
Most green-card applicants will need to go abroad to apply for permanent residency at an American consulate, rather than filing from within the U.S. as they do now
